title: Undercut-threat and traffic-window features from nearest rivals
summary: F1 strategy literature emphasizes that undercut success depends on rival proximity, traffic, tyre warm-up, and safety-car exposure, not just own tyre age.
rationale: F1 strategy literature emphasizes that undercut success depends on rival proximity, traffic, tyre warm-up, and safety-car exposure, not just own tyre age. Public strategy analyses repeatedly note that a stop is attractive when fresh-tyre out-lap can jump the nearby car, but can fail if traffic or poor warm-up blocks the move.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year x LapNumber`, compute leakage-safe local context: nearest car ahead/behind by position, local TyreLife/LapTime_Delta differences to those rivals, number of cars within +/-2 positions on same compound, same-lap pit-rate among nearby rivals, and 'clean-air after pit' proxies using gaps in position order or race-lap pace tiers. Interact these with compound and late-race flags.
expected_effect: Captures attack/defend pit behavior that fixed compound-finish windows miss, especially for midfield cars where relative position matters most.
risk: The dataset lacks explicit time gaps, so proxy traffic features may be noisy; some race-lap context can accidentally peek at future information if built from unsorted sequences or fold-global aggregates.
